errors: [The lookback periods for LDL, HDL, smoking status, and CVDRiskAssessment are set to 6 years, which may not align with standard clinical practice for primary prevention guidelines. Typically, more recent data (e.g., 1-2 years) are used for risk assessment. Additionally, the "MostRecentSmokingStatus" and "MostRecentCVDRiskAssessment" logic references an undefined ValueSet ("?"), which could lead to incomplete or incorrect data retrieval.]
["ValueSet 'Reason for not performing Chlamydia Screening' is marked as TBD and requires specific clinical codes to document screening deferrals"]

The lookback period for Colonoscopy is set to 1 month, which is clinically unreasonable for colon cancer screening. Clinical guidelines typically recommend screening intervals of 5-10 years for average-risk patients, not 1 month. This mismatch would lead to incorrect identification of patients needing screening.

[Time Interval for ProcedureLookBack is set to 1 month, but prostate cancer screening guidelines typically recommend intervals of 1-2 years for average-risk patients. The 1-month lookback period would trigger unnecessary frequent screening, which is not clinically reasonable.]

[HbA1c Laboratory Test threshold of 9 g% is clinically unreasonable. Standard diagnostic thresholds for Type 2 Diabetes typically use HbA1c ≥ 6.5% (per ADA guidelines). The 9 g% threshold would significantly delay diagnosis and underdiagnose cases.]

[The ELM implementation is missing the 20 pack-year smoking history criterion required by the CPG. The "Has Smoking History" logic only checks for active tobacco use (via ValueSet) but does not verify the 20 pack-year duration, which is a critical component of the USPSTF recommendation.]

[Age thresholds are set to 60-75 years instead of the standard 45-75 years for average-risk colorectal cancer screening. The 2-year lookback period for recent screening does not align with standard intervals (e.g., 10 years for colonoscopy), potentially leading to premature or unnecessary repeat screenings.]
[Age thresholds in ELM (30-65) do not match CPG recommendations (21-65). The lower age limit should be 21, not 30, according to USPSTF guidelines.]

[Age threshold in In Demographic is set to 75 years, but the USPSTF rationale references 65 years or older for falls prevention recommendations. This creates a discrepancy between the implemented age criteria (75) and the clinical guideline basis (65).]
[Age threshold in ELM is set to 25 years, but the CPG (USPSTF) recommends screening for adults 18 years or older. This discrepancy excludes eligible patients aged 18-24 from the target population.]
[Age thresholds in ELM (55-75 years) do not match CPG guidance (65-75 years for men who have ever smoked). The In Demographic logic should specify age >= 65 instead of 55.]

[MeetsInclusionCriteria incorrectly uses "OR" between Screening Age and Gender instead of "AND", leading to inclusion of patients who are either female (regardless of age) or over 40 (regardless of gender). The correct clinical logic should require both female gender AND age >=40 for inclusion.]

[Needs Tobacco Use Screening logic does not exclude patients with recent tobacco screening. The ELM defines Needs Tobacco Use Screening as [In Demographic] (age >=18) without incorporating the [Has Recent Tobacco Screening] condition. This would incorrectly include patients who have already been screened in the past year, conflicting with standard practice of avoiding redundant screenings.]

[Age threshold only includes lower bound (50 years) but lacks upper bound (80 years) as per CPG. Missing pack-year smoking history requirement. Tobacco use status checks only 'active' status but does not account for former smokers who quit within specified timeframe (typically 15 years).]

[Time Interval for Colonoscopy Lookback is 1 month, but clinical guidelines typically recommend a 10-year interval for colonoscopy screening. The 1-month lookback period does not align with standard screening intervals.]

[MeetsExclusionCriteria incorrectly uses AND instead of OR for Connective Tissue Disorders and Amiodarones. Exclusion criteria should require exclusion if either condition is present, not both. This logical error would fail to exclude patients with either connective tissue disease or amiodarone use, which are both established causes of interstitial lung disease and contraindications for IPF evaluation.]
